response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his background centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project, with no evidence of MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial-automation domain knowledge. The systems-level DLang work shows transferable low-latency skills, but the gap on core requirements is too large for a mid-senior C++ leadership role.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014–2015); role demands current, deep C++/STL expertise
No exposure to industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), SDL, MQTT, or automation protocols
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, diverging from industrial C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 background
Strong general systems and low-latency engineering skills
